file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 10/10 (0 FAIL, 0 WARN, 6 PASS)

rules.detail: The file is well-formed JSON. No trailing commas, no comments, all keys and string values are properly double-quoted, and brackets are balanced correctly.
No passwords, API keys, tokens, or any sensitive data present. The file contains only TypeScript compiler directives and a glob include pattern.

All keys use camelCase consistently (compilerOptions, moduleResolution, esModuleInterop, skipLibCheck, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames, resolveJsonModule, isolatedModules, noEmit, include). This matches the TypeScript tsconfig.json specification with no mixed conventions.
Maximum nesting depth is 3 levels (root → compilerOptions → individual option values), well within the 5-level threshold. Evaluated leniently per tsconfig.json rules.

All keys are fully descriptive and self-documenting. No single-letter keys or cryptic abbreviations are used. Keys like forceConsistentCasingInFileNames and isolatedModules clearly communicate their purpose.
The file uses consistent 2-space indentation throughout all 15 lines. It is not minified; each key-value pair occupies its own line. Formatting is clean and idiomatic for a tsconfig.json.
